name: clawddocs
tagline: ClawHub (301★): Clawdbot documentation expert with decision tree navigation, search scripts, doc fetching, version tracking, and config sni
verdict: Main risk: every step depends on live infrastructure -- fetching docs, qmd-based indexing, cache refresh -- with zero guidance on what to do if a fetch fails, qmd is missing, or a script errors; a broken dependency could stall or silently corrupt the docs state. Security is clean though.

highlights.summary: Multiple network/tool-dependent steps (doc fetching, qmd-based indexing, cache refresh) with no mention anywhere of what to do if a fetch fails, qmd is missing, or a script errors.
No 'NOT for' / out-of-scope / pointer-to-other-skill text appears anywhere in description or body.
No version field in frontmatter, no changelog section, no dated revision notes for the skill itself anywhere in the file (the 'Version Tracking' section tracks Clawdbot's own docs,
